summary: DHH expresses frustration with IBM's hard drive reliability issues, noting a new 8-hour/day usage guideline for their 120GXP series. He references his own bad experience with a failure-prone 75GXP drive that sparked a class-action lawsuit, and an IBM laptop drive that caused previous Sony laptop problems. He declares he's done buying IBM storage products.
key_points: IBM issued an 8-hour/day usage guideline for 120GXP drives, undermining confidence in their reliability
The 75GXP series was so failure-prone it triggered a class-action lawsuit
DHH's personal experience with multiple IBM drive failures cemented his distrust
money_quotes: What is this company thinking about?
I used to hold IBM in high regard when it came to harddrives. That's definatly long gone.
This company is not getting my data storage business for a decade.
key_insight: Brand loyalty evaporates quickly when a company ships unreliable hardware and responds with usage restrictions instead of fixes.
